entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The downy mildew pathogen that causes disease in pearl millet.

entities.name: PENNISETUM AMERICANUM
entities.description: The host plant, commonly known as pearl millet.

entities.name: ASEXUAL SPORULATION
entities.description: The process of producing asexual spores by the pathogen.

entities.name: RELATIVE HUMIDITY
entities.description: Atmospheric moisture levels, specifically high RH (95-100%) essential for sporulation.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal environment affecting the rate and size of sporulation structures.

entities.name: FLUORESCENT LIGHT
entities.description: A light source under which sporulation occurs.

entities.name: DARKNESS
entities.description: The absence of light under which sporulation occurs.

entities.name: NEAR-UV LIGHT
entities.description: Ultraviolet light source under which sporulation occurs.

entities.name: SPORANGIA SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the sporangia produced by the pathogen.

entities.name: SPORANGIOPHORE SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the sporangiophores produced by the pathogen.
